title: rich indulgent midnight tunisian craving tagine
description: a rich, glassy-jelly tunisian dish featuring pink food colouring and char-grill-yang flavour, served on a cedar plank. inspired by tunisian cuisine, this dish is paleo-friendly and perfect for a midnight craving.
instructions: heat the olive oil in a large skillet over high heat and sear the beef short ribs until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
remove the short ribs from the skillet and set aside.
add the sliced ginger to the skillet and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
add the minced garlic to the skillet and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
add the pomegranate molasses, smoked paprika, cinnamon, and cayenne pepper to the skillet and stir to combine.
return the short ribs to the skillet and stir to coat with the spice mixture.
transfer the short ribs and spice mixture to a cedar plank and arrange the short ribs in a single layer.
brush the top of the short ribs with the lemon juice and honey.
char-grill the short ribs over high heat, using a grill or broiler, until caramelized and slightly charred, about 5 minutes per side.
remove the short ribs from the heat and let rest for 5 minutes before serving.
